name: Azure Service Bus API
what_it_does: Azure Service Bus is an enterprise message broker with queues and pub/sub topics — supports sessions, dead-lettering, scheduled messages, transactions, and AMQP protocol for reliable message delivery.
use_cases: Reliable task queuing for agent workflows requiring guaranteed exactly-once processing
Event fan-out via Service Bus Topics with multiple competing-consumer subscriptions
Ordered message processing via Service Bus Sessions for customer-specific workflows
Scheduled message delivery for time-delayed agent tasks
Dead-letter queue handling for failed agent message processing with poison message isolation
not_for: High-throughput real-time streaming (Azure Event Hubs is better for millions of events/sec)
Teams not on Azure (use SQS/SNS for AWS, Pub/Sub for GCP, or Kafka for cloud-agnostic)
Simple queue workloads where Azure Storage Queues would suffice
best_when: Your Azure-based agent architecture needs enterprise-grade messaging with ordered delivery, transactions, dead-lettering, and competing consumers.
avoid_when: You need streaming analytics or are not on Azure — Service Bus is for point-to-point and pub/sub messaging, not high-throughput event streams.

auth.notes: Azure AD (managed identity recommended) or Shared Access Signature (SAS) tokens. RBAC roles: Service Bus Data Sender, Data Receiver, Data Owner. Managed Identity for Azure-hosted agents eliminates credentials.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; Basic tier starts at $0.05 per million operations
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic: $0.05/M ops; Standard: $9.81/month + $0.01/M ops; Premium: $677/month per messaging unit
pricing.notes: Basic tier lacks topics (queues only). Standard has topics, sessions. Premium required for geo-redundancy and VNet integration.

performance.rate_limits: 1,000 concurrent connections per namespace (Standard); Premium: configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Azure SDK provides typed exceptions with clear message and ErrorCode. Dead-letter reason codes are standardized. AMQP error conditions map to specific exception types. Connection errors include retry guidance in exception properties.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Sessions enable ordered, exactly-once semantics. Message deduplication window (default 10 minutes) prevents duplicate sends. MessageId-based dedup at namespace level.
known_agent_gotchas: Message lock expiration causes message to reappear if not completed within lock duration — agents must complete or renew lock before processing timeout
Topics require Standard or Premium tier — Basic tier only has queues; wrong tier causes feature-not-available errors
Sessions are exclusive — only one consumer can process a session at a time; agents must release sessions explicitly
Service Bus namespaces have global DNS — namespace names must be globally unique, not just regionally
Peek vs Receive are different — PeekMessage does not lock; ReceiveAndDelete/PeekLock changes message availability

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure RBAC with managed identity (no credentials). SAS token scoped to specific queues/topics. Message encryption at rest. Private endpoints for network isolation. HIPAA, ISO 27001, SOC 2.
